label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The passage starts by saying, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So the key points here are that she just got her bachelor's degree with honors, even though she was working two jobs and being a wife and mother. The options are A through D. Option A says she became a good wife and got two jobs. Wait, but the passage mentions she had two jobs and was a wife and mother, but it's not about becoming a good wife. The happiness is about receiving the degree. So A might not be correct. Option B is she had just received her bachelor's degree. That's exactly what the author mentions as the reason for the happiness. The first sentence sets that as the context. So B is a strong candidate. Option C says she was a teacher at Ashland University. No, the passage says she was graduating from Ashland University and was about to start a teaching career. She didn't work there as a teacher yet. So C is incorrect. Option D is she was proud to be loved by her family. While her family was there for the graduation, the main reason for her happiness was the degree, not the love from family. So D might
